← All projects Case study — P.02 · Professional services · Automation

Compass

The best business development reaches the right person, at the right time, with the right message. Compass automates all three — and keeps the professional’s judgment in the loop.

Role Founder & Engineer Status In active development Market $700B+ professional services

The problem

Lawyers, consultants, and other professional services providers know business development is critical — but it competes with billable work for their scarcest resource. Today’s workflow: manually scan news, filings, and reports; research each prospect; draft outreach from scratch; repeat daily. In practice, it slips for weeks.

Rainmaking still runs on manual effort and luck. The partners who generate business are the ones who happen to notice a relevant filing — and most don’t, because they’re busy billing.

The system

1 · Signal Library

A modular event-monitoring engine that detects commercial triggers — court filings, funding rounds, executive hires, M&A activity — and enriches raw events with who’s involved, what’s at stake, and why it matters. New signal types plug in without rebuilding the platform.

2 · R-Score

A relevance-ranking algorithm that surfaces the highest-value prospects first: signal recency (50%) because timely outreach dramatically improves response rates, industry match (30%) because target-industry prospects convert better, and decision-maker authority (20%) because reaching the right person beats reaching any person.

3 · Hook Generator

AI-drafted opening lines that reference the specific trigger event, tailored to the prospect’s industry and situation — outreach that reads as informed, not automated.

4 · Daily Digest

A human-in-the-loop review surface: a curated set of drafted emails each day with approve / edit / reject controls and one-click sending. The professional keeps control; the drafting burden disappears.

Why it’s built this way

Compass’s pipeline is an early agentic architecture: a system that monitors external sources, enriches what it finds, scores it against criteria, and drafts actions for review. The human review step is a guardrail, not a limitation — as the system builds a track record of accurate scoring, review can be progressively automated away. That progression from tool to agent is the arc I’m designing toward, and it’s the subject of Agentic Systems Are the Next Infrastructure Layer.

The platform also scales horizontally: court dockets for litigators, funding rounds for venture lawyers, M&A for corporate advisors, regulatory changes for compliance consultants. Each vertical is a wedge into a new segment with the same core engine.

Status

The MVP is functional — signal processing, relevance scoring, hook generation, and the daily digest all operational. Next phases: real-time data-source integration, email delivery, and analytics.
